subtasks.description: Query budget data aggregated by product category with cost trends over time (month-over-month or year-over-year). Include total spend, average spend per category, and trend calculations (variance, growth rate) to support allocation analysis.

Reconcile budget allocation analysis with cost trends, classify spending patterns (high-growth, declining, stable), assess efficiency gaps, and generate reallocation recommendations based on ROI potential and organizational priorities.

plan.cost_rationale: Two-subtask decomposition: (1) sql_gen (mid tier) for multi-table budget aggregation with trend calculations across time periods and product categories—mid tier handles joins and window functions for growth rate calculation. (2) cross_recon (frontier tier) for strategic reconciliation: takes structured budget data and generates classification of spending patterns, efficiency assessment, and reallocation recommendations. Frontier is required because this involves multi-signal analysis (spend levels + trends + priorities) and inference-based prioritization, not just data retrieval. No separate policy_action needed—governance alignment is part of the reconciliation decision-making. No separate multitool_plan needed—the analysis uses SQL data only, not multi-tool orchestration.

output.sql: WITH monthly_spend AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)::DATE AS month_date, cf.product, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS monthly_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS active_users FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.event_date >= '2023-01-01' AND cf.event_date <= '2024-12-31' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)::DATE, cf.product), product_metrics AS (SELECT ms.product, ms.month_date, ms.monthly_spend, ms.active_users, LAG(ms.monthly_spend) OVER (PARTITION BY ms.product ORDER BY ms.month_date) AS prev_month_spend, SUM(ms.monthly_spend) OVER (PARTITION BY ms.product) AS total_spend FROM monthly_spend ms), trend_analysis AS (SELECT pm.product, pm.month_date, pm.monthly_spend, pm.active_users, pm.total_spend, CASE WHEN pm.prev_month_spend IS NOT NULL THEN ROUND(((pm.monthly_spend - pm.prev_month_spend) / pm.prev_month_spend * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) ELSE NULL END AS mom_growth_rate, ROUND(pm.monthly_spend / NULLIF(pm.active_users, 0)::NUMERIC, 2) AS spend_per_user FROM product_metrics pm), category_summary AS (SELECT ta.product, COUNT(DISTINCT ta.month_date) AS months_tracked, SUM(ta.monthly_spend) AS total_category_spend, ROUND(AVG(ta.monthly_spend)::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_monthly_spend, ROUND(STDDEV_POP(ta.monthly_spend)::NUMERIC, 2) AS spend_variance, MIN(ta.monthly_spend) AS min_monthly_spend, MAX(ta.monthly_spend) AS max_monthly_spend, ROUND(AVG(ta.mom_growth_rate)::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_mom_growth_pct FROM trend_analysis ta GROUP BY ta.product) SELECT cs.product, cs.months_tracked, cs.total_category_spend, cs.avg_monthly_spend, cs.spend_variance, cs.min_monthly_spend, cs.max_monthly_spend, cs.avg_mom_growth_pct, ROUND((cs.total_category_spend / SUM(cs.total_category_spend) OVER ())::NUMERIC * 100, 2) AS spend_pct_of_total FROM category_summary cs ORDER BY cs.total_category_spend DESC
